name: Dobosh Torte
recipeInstructions:
CAKE: Beat the egg yolks at medium speed until they are lemon-colored and thick. Add the sugar and continue to beat until well blended. Sift the baking powder into the mixture. Then fold it into the egg yolk mixture. Beat the egg whites until stiff, then fold them gently into the mixture. Grease two round pans if making four layers, or two loaf pans if making seven. Line the bottoms with wax paper that has been greased. Pour in the batter and bake at 350°F for 15-20 minutes or until a tester comes out clean. Turn out immediately and remove the wax paper. Cool on racks with the top side up. Make thin slices when cool, frosting with the cut side up, then layering. Cover the entire torte with buttercream and melt down semi-sweet chocolate to melting point, stirring well. Chill the cake and when firm pour over the dark chocolate making sure that the sides are covered. Chill to harden. Remove to a new plate so drips are not seen.
FROSTING: Mix sugar, water, and cream of tartar in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Boil over medium heat until 234°F on a candy thermometer. In a large bowl with a mixer beat the eggs until thick. Add the butter in small pieces and beat well. Combine chocolate and coffee in a saucepan and heat gently until chocolate is melted and mixed. Add this and the vanilla to the bowl and beat well. If the buttercream starts not holding up, refrigerate for a few minutes then beat again.
